expectations.text: Lists at least three plausible state differences (different params name, missing populate, different upstream restriction, env / version drift) without committing to one cause
expectations.evidence: Lists 4 numbered candidates (params_name, populate didn't run, upstream restriction, env/version drift); explicitly 'Don't commit to a single cause yet'.

expectations.text: Recommends a concrete first diagnostic (e.g., inspecting (Selection & key).fetch() output side-by-side)
expectations.evidence: 'First thing to actually do' shows `(Selection & key).fetch(as_dict=True)` and `(ComputedTable & key).fetch(as_dict=True)` side by side.

expectations.text: Fetches from UserEnvironment and compares against the user's running env (sys.version, package versions); does not generic-suggest 'check your env'.
expectations.evidence: Concrete code fetches `(UserEnvironment & {'env_id': my_env_id}).fetch1('env')` and compares with `sys.version`/pip freeze rather than generic suggestion.

expectations.text: Does not claim the pipeline state must be identical when the symptoms prove otherwise
expectations.evidence: '"Spyglass must be deterministic so the states must be identical": the empty fetch is itself the disproof. Trust the symptom.'

expectations.text: Does not misframe cautious_delete as a write/populate gate — cautious_delete blocks deletes only
expectations.evidence: 'cautious_delete: it's a delete-time safety gate ... does not block writes, does not block populate()'.
